title: Daily Market Digest: Geopolitical Tensions & Tech Sell-off Weigh on Indices
subtitle: July 20, 2026 – Early Market Snapshot

items.headline: US Futures Rise Ahead of Trading Session Amidst Escalating Middle East Conflict and Anticipated Big Tech Earnings
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed gains this morning as investors weighed continued geopolitical tensions in the Middle East against a busy week of major technology earnings reports. Oil prices remained volatile, with Brent crude dipping slightly but staying elevated. Last week saw major indices close lower, largely due to a sell-off in chip stocks.

items.headline: S&P 500
items.statContext: S&P 500 futures climbed ahead of the Monday session.

items.headline: NASDAQ 100
items.statContext: Nasdaq 100 futures also advanced this morning.

items.headline: Dow Jones Industrial Average
items.statContext: Dow Jones Industrial Average futures posted modest gains.

items.headline: S&P 500 (Friday Close)
items.statContext: The S&P 500 closed 1.55% lower last week.

items.headline: NASDAQ 100 (Friday Close)
items.statContext: The Nasdaq 100 saw a significant drop of 4.13% last week, driven by technology names.

items.headline: Dow Jones (Friday Close)
items.statContext: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.93% for the week.

items.headline: Biggest Gainer
items.summary: Alibaba Group Holding Limited surged 5% after showcasing a preview of its Qwen 3.8 Max AI model, described as a strong competitor in the AI coding model market.

items.headline: Significant Gainer
items.summary: Fervo Energy shares gained 5% following a 'Buy' upgrade from Jefferies, citing an attractive entry point after a recent 40% pullback from its IPO highs.

items.headline: Notable Gainer
items.summary: Yeti Holdings saw a 2% increase after Goldman Sachs upgraded the stock to 'Buy,' anticipating a more durable growth outlook.

items.headline: Biggest Loser
items.summary: Intuitive Surgical experienced a sharp decline of 14.1%, making it the weakest performer in the S&P 500.

items.headline: Significant Loser
items.summary: Cadence Design Systems was among the top S&P 500 losers with a 6.5% drop.

items.headline: Notable Loser
items.summary: Synopsys shares fell 5.8% today, contributing to the broader tech sector's struggles.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Intensify, Driving Oil Prices Above $90
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, with renewed U.S. military strikes on Iran and concerns over shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, has kept oil prices highly volatile, pushing Brent crude past $90 per barrel. This has led to a cautious market sentiment.

items.headline: Tech Sector Under Pressure as AI Capital Expenditure Concerns Rise
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chipmakers, has faced significant selling pressure. The Nasdaq 100 experienced broad losses last week, and the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index entered a technical bear market, falling over 20% from its June highs. This comes amidst skepticism about the immediate returns on massive artificial intelligence capital expenditures.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports on Deck for the Week Ahead
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting a slew of earnings reports this week. Key companies scheduled to report include Alphabet ($GOOGL), Tesla ($TSLA), Intel ($INTC), and IBM ($IBM), with many reporting after the close on Wednesday.

items.headline: Energy Sector Outperforms Amidst Broader Market Weakness
items.summary: Bucking the trend of broader market declines, the energy sector emerged as a leading performer, benefiting from strong oil prices driven by geopolitical instability. This signals a rotation into more defensive sectors.

items.headline: Economic Data Releases: Inflation and Employment in Focus
items.summary: This week's economic calendar includes important inflation data from Canada and New Zealand, as well as UK inflation figures. The U.S. will also see the release of State Employment and Unemployment data for June and the Usual Weekly Earnings for the second quarter.
